items.headline: CBP Confirms April 20 Launch Date for CAPE Refund System
items.summary: CBP published CSMS #68315804 confirming Phase 1 of the CAPE system will launch April 20, 2026. The system will handle IEEPA duty refunds through the ACE portal, limited to unliquidated entries and entries within 80 days of liquidation. Refunds expected within 60-90 days of accepted declarations.

items.headline: CAPE System Launch Details: CSV Files, 9,999 Entry Limit Per Declaration
items.summary: CBP released comprehensive guidance showing CAPE declarations will be submitted via CSV file upload through ACE Portal, limited to 9,999 entries per declaration. Only importers of record or authorized brokers who filed the entries can submit claims. Electronic ACH enrollment mandatory for refunds.

items.headline: CBP Publishes CAPE Reference Guide and Resources Ahead of Launch
items.summary: CBP published multiple resources including a fact sheet, CAPE Information Notice, step-by-step reference guide, and dedicated webpage to support the April 20 launch. The materials clarify that Phase 1 covers approximately 63% of IEEPA entries with more complex scenarios in later phases.

items.headline: CBP Updates Refund Timeline: 45 Days vs 60-90 Days Processing
items.summary: CBP's March 31 declaration to CIT indicated 45-day processing timeline for accepted CAPE declarations, while public guidance suggests 60-90 days. The discrepancy appears related to compliance review requirements, with the shorter timeline applying when no compliance concerns exist.

items.headline: CBP Files Status Update on Finally Liquidated Entries Refund Process
items.summary: In response to CIT's March 27 order requiring refunds for finally liquidated entries, CBP confirmed Phase 1 cannot handle these cases. A subsequent phase will address finally liquidated entries but no timeline provided. CBP emphasized Phase 1 focuses on unliquidated and recently liquidated entries only.

items.headline: CIT Expands Refund Order to Include Finally Liquidated Entries
items.summary: Judge Richard Eaton amended his order requiring CBP to reliquidate and refund IEEPA tariffs even for entries beyond final liquidation where the 180-day protest period has expired. This significantly expands the scope of required refunds beyond CBP's initial Phase 1 capabilities.

items.headline: 26,000+ Importers Enrolled in Electronic Refunds Representing $120B
items.summary: More than 26,000 importers of record have enrolled in electronic refund systems, representing 78% of impacted entries and over $120 billion in duty value. This high enrollment rate suggests strong industry readiness for the CAPE system launch and indicates significant refund volume ahead.
